_README: Synthetic-data Optuna optimization results (3 asset classes, 2 optimizer seeds each, out-of-sample validated). These are DIRECTIONAL recommendations: the synthetic generator trends cleaner than real markets, so the structure/direction of these settings is trustworthy but the exact floats and the headline win-rates/PF are optimistic. Re-validate on real candles via the Render backtest before relying on them live. This file is documentation only and is NOT auto-loaded by settings (that would be config/optimized_params.json).

method.entry_model: EMA crossover + strength threshold on synthetic OHLCV (NOT the production fusion entry)

applies_to_production.directional_only_entry_keys: EMA_FAST/EMA_SLOW/ENTRY_THR/ATR_GATE_MULT are specific to the harness's EMA entry and do NOT map to the production fusion entry. Their lesson translates only as: be more selective (raise FUSION_THRESHOLD / MIN_ATR_NORM, only trade adequate volatility).

crypto.verdict: Momentum has a clear edge on trending crypto. Let winners run.
key_insights: Let winners run: distant TP1/TP2 + long max-duration win.
Do NOT trail before TP1; trail loosely after (ratchet ~2.0 ATR).
Be selective on entries + use a mid-volatility gate.
Wide stops (~2.2-2.7 ATR) are fine given the far targets.

stocks.verdict: Marginally tradable; tighter stops + selectivity. Gaps punish over-trading.
key_insights: Trail BEFORE TP1 here (opposite of crypto) to capture upward drift.
Keep stops tight (~1.3 ATR): gaps make wide stops expensive.
Selectivity beats frequency; over-trading collapses out-of-sample.
Loose ratchet + wide TP2 to ride drift trends.

forex.verdict: NOT robustly tradable with a momentum entry (mean-reverting). Negative out-of-sample.
key_insights: High win-rate is a trap: avg loss 2-4x avg win (negative skew).
Take profit FAST (TP1 ~0.6 ATR); reversion eats far targets.
If pursued, use a mean-reversion entry, not momentum.
Maximum selectivity is the only lever that keeps it near break-even.

cross_asset_insight: Optimal pre-TP1 trailing is asset-dependent: OFF for crypto (let strong trends run), ON for stocks/forex (capture drift / cut reversion). Universal wins across all three: drop early-kill, keep the post-TP1 ratchet loose, gate on volatility, and prefer selectivity over trade count.
